quick_facts.label: Recruitment category
quick_facts.value: Commercial Cum Ticket Clerk (CCTC) is an RRB Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) undergraduate post, listed at Pay Level 3 in the 7th Central Pay Commission pay matrix.

quick_facts.label: Minimum education
quick_facts.value: In CEN 06/2024, the prescribed qualification was 12th (+2 stage) or equivalent with at least 50% aggregate marks; the 50% condition was waived for SC/ST/PwBD/ex-servicemen candidates and candidates holding qualifications higher than 12th.

quick_facts.label: Selection route
quick_facts.value: For CCTC, CEN 06/2024 specified two computer-based tests, followed by document verification and a Railway medical examination; unlike clerical-typist posts, it did not specify a typing-skill test for CCTC.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The CCTC post was notified with medical standard B2 in CEN 06/2024. Candidates must meet the applicable Railway medical-fitness requirements for the notified post.

quick_facts.label: Entry basic pay context
quick_facts.value: The CEN 06/2024 notification stated an initial basic pay of ₹21,700 for CCTC at Pay Level 3. This is a dated basic-pay figure; actual monthly gross and take-home pay vary with applicable allowances, place of posting, deductions, and later pay revisions.

quick_facts.label: Training
quick_facts.value: Indian Railways' published initial/promotion module for Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerks is 60 working days (360 hours), covering coaching and goods work, accounts, safety, IT, soft skills, field training, and examination.

sections.heading: What the role covers
paragraphs: A Commercial Cum Ticket Clerk works within Indian Railways' Commercial Department, whose functions include selling railway services, passenger amenities, ticketing, collection and accounting of traffic receipts, and passenger-facing service. At the post level, this points to frontline commercial and passenger-service work rather than train operation or engineering.

The published CCTC training curriculum includes coaching theory and practical work, goods theory and practical work, accounts, safety management, information technology, and field training. Accordingly, the role requires staff to apply ticketing and commercial procedures, use the relevant systems, handle records and money accurately where assigned, and provide courteous, rule-based assistance to passengers.
bullets: Work may involve ticketing and related passenger-commercial services under local Railway rules and the assigned office or station.
The precise mix of counter, station, ticket-checking-support, accounts, or other commercial duties is determined by the Railway administration and posting.

sections.heading: How to enter through RRB NTPC
paragraphs: The external-entry route is an RRB Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) for NTPC undergraduate posts when CCTC vacancies are notified. The candidate applies online to one chosen RRB and must satisfy the post parameters in that particular CEN, including education, age, nationality, medical standard, reservation documentation where applicable, and other conditions.

The CEN 06/2024 pattern for CCTC was first-stage CBT, second-stage CBT, document verification, and medical examination. Appointment is not made by the RRB itself: the CEN states that RRBs recommend empanelled candidates, while the relevant Railway Administration offers appointment after the prescribed checks.

Existing Railway staff may also enter the grade through departmental selection where a Railway administration notifies such a quota; for example, South Central Railway published a CCTC Level-3 selection under an LDCE quota. This is a separate internal route, not a substitute for an open RRB notification.
bullets: Use only the official RRB websites and the relevant detailed CEN for live eligibility, vacancy, fee, dates, category-wise conditions, and examination instructions.
Do not assume that dates, vacancy totals, age relaxations, or the selection timetable from CEN 06/2024 apply to a future recruitment cycle.

sections.heading: Skills developed and used
paragraphs: The formal training modules indicate the core capability areas: passenger/coaching commercial knowledge, goods-commercial knowledge, accounts, safety, information technology, ethical leadership and soft skills, and supervised field practice. These support accurate, customer-facing work in a safety-conscious public-service setting.

Practical strengths for aspirants include clear communication with a diverse travelling public, numerical and record-keeping accuracy, comfort with computerised ticketing and office systems, calm handling of queues or service issues, and disciplined compliance with fares, concessions, accounting, and safety procedures.
bullets: Computer familiarity matters: the initial course includes an information-technology module, and Indian Railways' commercial functions use passenger systems such as PRS and UTS.
A typing test was not prescribed for CCTC in CEN 06/2024, but computer use and accurate data entry remain relevant operational skills.

sections.heading: Posting and work environment
paragraphs: CCTC vacancies are notified for Indian Railways' zonal railways and production units, so candidates should be prepared for assignment within the Railway/RRB allocation reflected in the recruitment notice and appointment process. The broader Commercial Department provides ticketing, passenger services, claims and commercial functions across the railway system.

In practice, the work is public-facing and may be stationed at railway offices, booking or ticketing locations, and other commercial establishments. Official staff records show CCTC and Senior Commercial Cum Ticket Clerk personnel posted under ticket-checking staff at Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus, illustrating that actual workplace assignment can vary within the commercial organisation.
bullets: Expect rule-driven work, public interaction, use of commercial systems, and local supervisory arrangements.
Posting location, duties, shift arrangements, and transfer liability must be checked in the applicable CEN, Railway rules, and appointment terms rather than assumed from the job title.

sections.heading: Progression and professional development
paragraphs: Indian Railways' published Commercial training framework separately provides for CCTC initial/promotion training and for Senior Commercial Cum Ticket Clerk initial training. This documents a departmental development path into senior commercial-clerk work, although promotion is not automatic and depends on the applicable service rules, eligibility, vacancies, selection process, and Railway administration.

A Senior Commercial Cum Ticket Clerk has also been notified as a distinct higher-level NTPC graduate post at Pay Level 5 in Railway recruitment material. Candidates should treat this as evidence of a related senior role, not as a guarantee that every CCTC will progress through the same route or timeline.
bullets: Use departmental notifications and current Railway establishment rules for promotion eligibility and quotas.
The published refresher module for Commercial-cum-Ticket Clerks is mandatory every two years for the covered lower-grade combined cadre, supporting continuing operational learning.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: CEN 06/2024 placed CCTC at Level 3 of the 7th CPC pay matrix with an initial basic pay of ₹21,700. This should be read as the notification's entry basic-pay context, rather than as a current in-hand salary promise.

Actual earnings can differ materially by date and posting because central-government pay components and deductions may change, and because house-rent, dearness, transport and other allowances depend on the applicable rules and circumstances. Candidates should verify current pay and allowances from their appointment documents and official Railway/Central Government orders.
bullets: Use basic pay, not unverified online “in-hand salary” estimates, for a reliable comparison of the notified post.
The notified CEN's age, vacancy, fee, and pay details are recruitment-cycle-specific and may be revised through corrigenda or future CENs.
